Real engagement · Gold's Gym × FitCopilot.ai · 2024

A new AI lead-gen system, deployed at a Gold's Gym franchise.

A pioneering AI-driven gym lead-generation deployment at Gold's Gym Newburgh/Middletown. Built through FitCopilot.ai (the AI agent platform Bill co-founded as the productization of the GTM patterns Treetop had been running with fitness clients). 16 unique data points captured per inbound prospect. Generative AI used to recommend specific equipment and program paths for each prospect once in-club. The MVP launch generated 120 qualified leads in a two-week window — and achieved the lowest cost-per-lead in the location's history.

Lowest CPL
in Gold's Gym Newburgh/Middletown history — at MVP scale, with the AI lead-gen system live

Named engagement, published with the consent of the participants. Specific cost-per-lead numbers aggregated for confidentiality; relative comparison ("lowest in location history") reflects the actual operator outcome.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy + Co-Founder, FitCopilot.ai
Published May 2026 · More case studies
The starting situation

A franchise location that was leaving qualified leads on the table

Operator: Gold's Gym Newburgh / Middletown — a multi-location franchise operation in the broader Gold's Gym network. Established membership base, good local presence, traditional fitness marketing motion.

The constraint: Like most franchise fitness locations, lead generation looked like every other gym's: paid social ads to a landing page with name + email + phone capture; sometimes a "fitness goals" dropdown. The data captured was thin, the prospect arrived for their tour cold, and the membership consultant had to do all the discovery work in person.

What FitCopilot.ai brought: A purpose-built AI lead-gen system that captures 16 unique data points about each new prospect through a conversational web-form-meets-quiz experience — and uses generative AI to produce equipment recommendations + program-path suggestions that the prospect sees immediately and the membership consultant sees in their pre-tour brief.

What we built

The AI lead-gen system

16-data-point conversational capture

Rather than a 3-field static form, the prospect goes through a conversational flow that captures: fitness experience level, primary goals (fat loss / muscle gain / sport-specific / general health), available training time per week, preferred training style, injury history, current activity level, motivation drivers, prior gym experience, dietary preferences, sleep + recovery context, and several other dimensions. The conversational format produces a 65-80% completion rate vs the 25-35% of static lead-capture forms in the same demographic.

Generative AI equipment + program-path recommendations

Once the 16 data points are captured, the system uses an LLM to produce: a recommended starting program (cardio / strength / hybrid / class-based), specific equipment to use on day one, a "if you came in tomorrow, here's what we'd put you on" narrative the prospect sees on-screen, and a coach-facing brief that the membership consultant reads before the tour. The prospect arrives warm — they already know what their plan looks like.

Pre-tour brief for the membership consultant

The membership consultant gets a 90-second pre-tour brief on each prospect: goals, experience, recommended program path, conversation hooks, likely objections. Tours convert at materially higher rates because the consultant skips the discovery step and goes straight to the program demonstration tailored to the prospect.

MVP launch results

  • 120 qualified leads generated in a two-week period at the Gold's Gym Newburgh/Middletown location during the MVP launch window.
  • Lowest cost-per-lead in the location's history — the combination of higher form-completion rate + better-qualified leads + the novelty of the conversational experience drove paid-social ad performance materially above the location's prior benchmarks.
  • Membership consultant time per qualified tour reduced by skipping the "tell me about yourself" discovery phase.
The bigger arc

From Treetop fractional engagement → FitCopilot.ai platform

The Gold's Gym deployment was the MVP launch for fitagentic.ai (formerly FitCopilot.ai), the AI agent platform Bill co-founded as the productization of GTM patterns he'd been running with Treetop fitness clients across years of fractional engagements.

The thesis: most fitness operators don't need a custom-built marketing function. They need a productized AI agent platform that does lead nurture, no-show recovery, member retention, and member-facing coaching by default — the way Hapana's enterprise CRM did class scheduling and member management by default. The Gold's Gym launch validated the demand: operators want the platform if it actually delivers the lead-quality lift it promises.

FitCopilot.ai has since:

  • Generated 5,000+ consumer fitness leads through aiworkoutgenerator.com — validating B2C demand for AI-driven fitness journey design and informing the segmentation for B2B operator commercialization.
  • Built operator ROI models demonstrating $8-$12 revenue impact per active member, used to drive multi-location interest.
  • Shaped product roadmap via primary research with industry-leading club operators to ascertain product-market fit for AI-driven member journey systems at scale.
What we'd tell you about this pattern

What worked, and what this means for franchise operators

What worked

  • Conversational lead capture outperforms static forms — materially. The 65-80% completion rate vs 25-35% for static forms is not a marginal lift. It's the difference between an effective acquisition motion and a leaky one. The novelty of the conversational format compounds with the data depth.
  • AI-generated equipment recommendations create a "wow" moment. Prospects arrive at the tour having already seen what their starting program might look like. The membership consultant becomes the validator rather than the salesperson — a materially better-converting conversation.
  • The pre-tour brief is the multiplier. Without the coach-facing brief, the same AI lead-capture is just a fancier form. The brief is where the operator-facing value compounds — every membership consultant becomes effectively senior overnight because the discovery work is done before they meet the prospect.

If you're a fitness operator (franchise or independent)

The Gold's Gym deployment is one location of a platform-scale opportunity. For franchise operators and independent club owners who want the same AI-native lead-gen + member journey infrastructure, the productized version is at fitagentic.ai. For broader context on the prospect-to-member AI architecture: prospect-to-member AI playbook and how to replace your gym front desk with AI agents.
